items.title: AI culture is becoming a public imagination problem.
items.tickerTitle: AI culture is now a public argument
items.summary: A weekly field note connecting model launches, regulation, creative tools and public language around machine intelligence.
items.xPost: AI culture is no longer just a technology story. It is becoming a public imagination problem: tools, rights, images, identity and power in one strange new surface.

items.title: Image models are becoming identity infrastructure.
items.summary: A short visual desk note about style, memory, profile images and generated presence.
items.xPost: Image models are becoming identity infrastructure: not just pictures, but profile surfaces, memory machines and style systems.

items.title: Agents are moving from impressive demos into ordinary routines.
items.tickerTitle: Agents move from demos into daily workflows
items.summary: A practical note about when delegation helps, when it fails and why interfaces matter.
items.xPost: Agents become interesting when they leave the demo and enter the routine. The question is not magic. It is delegation, friction and trust.

items.title: AI search puts source links back in the spotlight.
items.tickerTitle: AI search puts source links back in the spotlight
items.summary: A source-first brief about attribution, summaries and why links still matter.
items.xPost: AI search makes source trails feel newly important. Summaries are useful, but the link is still where context, responsibility and trust begin.

items.title: Creator style protection enters the policy debate.
items.tickerTitle: Creator style protection enters the policy debate
items.summary: A rights note about likeness, style, opt-outs and the culture around protection.
items.xPost: The style question is becoming a policy question: what can be protected, what can be referenced and what creators need from AI systems.
